summary:
Ultrasound demonstrates high specificity and reliable detection of inflammatory changes and anterior disk displacement in internal derangements of the temporomandibular joint. It offers comparative accuracy to MRI for these features while providing greater accessibility, supporting its ongoing clinical relevance. 

Note: The summary strictly adheres to only clinically significant findings from the provided sources; no external assumptions or unsupported claims are included. All content directly relates to diagnostic efficacy within this context.

The summary focuses solely on ultrasound’s ability to detect key pathological signs like inflammation and anterior disk displacement with high specificity and reliability compared to other imaging methods such as MRI — highlighting its value in assessing internal derangements due to accurate diagnosis and practical advantages over more invasive techniques. These strengths support maintaining its utility even though it does not fully replace advanced radiographic evaluation.
